meta.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
meta.message: Detected a potential path traversal. A malicious actor could control the location of this file, to include going backwards in the directory with '../'. To address this, ensure that user-controlled variables in file paths are sanitized. You may also consider using a utility method such as org.apache.commons.io.FilenameUtils.getName(...) to only retrieve the file name from the path.

meta.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
meta.message: Detected a request with potential user-input going into a OutputStream or Writer object. This bypasses any view or template environments, including HTML escaping, which may expose this application to cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities. Consider using a view technology such as JavaServer Faces (JSFs) which automatically escapes HTML views.

meta.cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength
meta.message: DES is considered deprecated. AES is the recommended cipher. Upgrade to use AES. See https://www.nist.gov/news-events/news/2005/06/nist-withdraws-outdated-data-encryption-standard for more information.

meta.cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength
meta.message: Triple DES (3DES or DESede) is considered deprecated. AES is the recommended cipher. Upgrade to use AES.

meta.cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength
meta.message: DES is considered deprecated. AES is the recommended cipher. Upgrade to use AES. See https://www.nist.gov/news-events/news/2005/06/nist-withdraws-outdated-data-encryption-standard for more information.

meta.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
meta.message: Detected input from a HTTPServletRequest going into a 'ProcessBuilder' or 'exec' command. This could lead to command injection if variables passed into the exec commands are not properly sanitized. Instead, avoid using these OS commands with user-supplied input, or, if you must use these commands, use a whitelist of specific values.

meta.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
meta.message: A formatted or concatenated string was detected as input to a ProcessBuilder call. This is dangerous if a variable is controlled by user input and could result in a command injection. Ensure your variables are not controlled by users or sufficiently sanitized.

meta.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

meta.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
meta.message: Detected input from a HTTPServletRequest going into a SQL sink or statement. This could lead to SQL injection if variables in the SQL statement are not properly sanitized. Use parameterized SQL queries or properly sanitize user input instead.

meta.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
meta.message: Detected a potential path traversal. A malicious actor could control the location of this file, to include going backwards in the directory with '../'. You may also consider using a utility method such as org.apache.commons.io.FilenameUtils.getName(...) to only retrieve the file name from the path.

meta.cwe: CWE-90: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
meta.message: Detected input from a HTTPServletRequest going into an LDAP query. This could lead to LDAP injection if the input is not properly sanitized, which could result in attackers modifying objects in the LDAP tree structure. Ensure data passed to an LDAP query is not controllable or properly sanitize the data.

meta.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
meta.message: Detected a formatted string in a SQL statement. This could lead to SQL injection if variables in the SQL statement are not properly sanitized. Use a prepared statements (java.sql.PreparedStatement) instead. You can obtain a PreparedStatement using 'connection.prepareStatement'.

meta.cwe: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
meta.message: Detected use of the functions `Math.random()` or `java.util.Random()`. These are both not cryptographically strong random number generators (RNGs). If you are using these RNGs to create passwords or secret tokens, use `java.security.SecureRandom` instead.
